1.name: False Urgency
1.description: Creating a false sense of urgency through countdowns or scarcity claims
1.example: Fake countdown timer, 'Only 2 left' when stock is plentiful

2.name: Basket Sneaking
2.description: Adding items to cart or checkout without user consent
2.example: Auto-added warranty or donation at checkout

3.name: Confirm Shaming
3.description: Using guilt or shame to influence user decisions
3.example: 'No, I don't want to save money' as opt-out text

4.name: Interface Interference
4.description: Manipulating UI to highlight desired actions and obscure alternatives
4.example: Large bright 'Accept' button vs tiny grey 'Decline' link

5.name: Forced Action
5.description: Pre-selecting options or requiring unrelated actions to proceed
5.example: Pre-checked insurance checkbox during flight booking

6.name: Drip Pricing
6.description: Revealing additional charges incrementally through the purchase flow
6.example: Convenience fee appearing only at payment page

7.name: Disguised Advertisement
7.description: Advertisements designed to look like organic content
7.example: Sponsored product styled identically to search results

8.name: Nagging
8.description: Persistent, repeated interruptions to pressure users
8.example: Repeated pop-ups asking to enable notifications

9.name: Subscription Trap
9.description: Making it easy to subscribe but difficult to cancel

10.name: Bait & Switch
10.description: Advertising one offer but delivering a different one
10.example: 'Free cancellation' on listing, fee charged at checkout

11.name: Trick Question
11.description: Confusing language designed to mislead users

12.name: Hidden Costs
12.description: Charges not revealed until final payment stage
12.example: Service tax shown only on payment page

13.name: Rogue Malware
13.description: Tricking users into downloading unwanted software
13.example: Download button that installs unrelated toolbar
